article_title: DAEMON Tools Lite Backdoored for 27 Days: Supply Chain Attack Targeted Government and Scientific Organizations in Russia, Belarus, and Thailand
summary: All claims verified; gbhackers.com 403 is not a factual concern as all claims attributed to it are corroborated by the primary Kaspersky SecureList source

content_preview.title: DAEMON Tools Lite Backdoored for 27 Days: Supply Chain Attack Targeted Government and Scientific Organizations in Russia, Belarus, and Thailand
content_preview.summary: Kaspersky found official DAEMON Tools Lite installers trojanized from April 8 to May 5, 2026, deploying a multi-stage backdoor to over a dozen targeted machines. CISA added CVE-2026-8398 to its KEV catalog on May 27.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The official installation packages for DAEMON Tools Lite, a widely used Windows disk-imaging and virtual drive utility developed by AVB Disc Soft, were silently trojanized for 27 days before discovery, distributing a multi-stage backdoor to systems across more than 100 countries. [Kaspersky researchers](https://securelist.com/tr/daemon-tools-backdoor/119654/) published their findings on May 6, 2026, revealing that versions 12.5.0.2421 through 12.5.0.2434 were compromised, with the f...

recommendations: Consider adding gbhackers.com and blog.daemon-tools.cc to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent 1027-word News article with comprehensive technical depth. Well-structured with Overview, Attack Scope, Execution, Malware Architecture, Vulnerability Classification, Vendor Response, and What We Don't Know sections. The Analysis section adds genuine editorial value by placing the incident in broader supply chain context. The practical remediation advice at the end is valuable.
editor_notes.source_verification: Six of seven sources verified from disk snapshots. source-3.html.gz (gbhackers.com) returned HTTP 403 and could not be archived — however, all claims attributed to gbhackers.com are independently corroborated by the primary Kaspersky SecureList source (source-0). Verified: source-0.html.gz (Kaspersky SecureList): confirmed April 8 start date, versions 12.5.0.2421–12.5.0.2434, DTHelper/DiscSoftBusServiceLite/DTShellHlp.exe binary list, CRT environment quote verbatim, March 27 C2 domain registration, envchk.exe, QUIC RAT on educational institution in Russia, WolfSSL, notepad.exe/conhost.exe injection, 'chiper' misspelling, Chinese-language strings, 'do not currently attribute' quote, 100+ countries, Russia/Belarus/Thailand targeting. source-1.html.gz (BleepingComputer): confirmed attack timeframe. source-2.html.gz (HelpNetSecurity): confirmed attack narrative. source-4.html.gz (Daemon Tools blog): confirmed 'unauthorized interference within our infrastructure' quote, 07:00 GMT notification time, 12-hour response, version 12.6.0.2445, Ultra/Pro unaffected. source-5.html.gz (NVD): confirmed CWE-506, CVSS 3.1 score 9.8 CRITICAL, CVSS 4.0 score 9.3 CRITICAL. source-6.html.gz (SecurityWeek): confirmed 10% organizational systems figure.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specific claims verified. The article correctly quotes Kaspersky verbatim throughout. The GBHackers-attributed claims (educational institution QUIC RAT recipient; 'chiper' misspelling) are independently confirmed in Kaspersky SecureList source. The version number discrepancy between the article (12.5.0.2421–12.5.0.2434) and the vendor blog (12.5.1) is a labeling difference, not a factual error — Kaspersky provides the precise build numbers.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Technical claims are properly attributed. The attribution uncertainty around Chinese-language strings is handled carefully and accurately.
editor_notes.originality: No prior DAEMON Tools supply chain article exists in the archive. The cross-reference to the Mini Shai-Hulud worm article is editorially appropriate.
recommendations: Consider adding gbhackers.com and blog.daemon-tools.cc to config/source_allowlist.txt
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Highly detailed, accurate cybersecurity article with strong sourcing across 7 outlets including the primary vendor and Kaspersky's research blog. Ready for publication.
